content.full_text:
May 6, 1997
John Ramirez
Nielsen, Merksamer, Parrinello,
Mueller & Naylor,
591 Redwood Highway
Mill Valley, California 94941
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 1-97-146
Dear Mr. Ramirez:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding provisions of the Political
Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
Since your advice request does not name a specific committee, we are
treating this as a request for informal assistance.
2
QUESTION
Is a committee that only participates in San Francisco elections considered a “city” or
“county” committee?
CONCLUSION
A conmiittee that only participates in San Francisco elections is considered a “county”
committee.
Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18100
-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329, subd. (c)(3), (c)(4).)


File No. 1-97-146
Page 2
ANALYSIS
The terms “City” and “County” are defined in the Act. City means a general law or a
chartered city. (Section 82008.) County includes a city and county. (Section 82017.) Since San
Francisco is not a general law or chartered city, but rather a consolidated city and county
municipality, committees formed to support or oppose only candidates andlor measures being
voted on in San Francisco are considered county committees. (Section 82027.5; see Moll Advice
Letter, No. A-97-080.)
Committees formed pursuant to subdivision (b) or (c) of Section 82013 (commonly
referred to as major donor and independent expenditure committees, respectively) are not
required to file regular preelection statements.
(Section 84200.5(d).)
3
Ifyou have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By: Lynda Cassady
Assistant Division Chief
Technical Assistance Division
3
However, county major donor and independent expenditure committees must file supplemental
independent expenditure reports on the City and County’s preelection filing schedule if they make independent
expenditures totaling $500 or more to support or oppose a single candidate or measure. (Section 84203.5.)
